subject: UX & Research
title: The Accessibility Overlay Controversy
description: One-minute skill: The Accessibility Overlay Controversy.

hook.text: Accessibility overlays promise one-line fixes: add a script and your site becomes compliant. Too good to be true.

buildup.text: Overlays add toolbars with font size, contrast, and spacing controls. They claim to fix compliance instantly.

discovery.text: Over 700 accessibility advocates signed an open letter against overlays, calling them fundamentally flawed.

twist.text: Overlays can make sites worse. They interfere with screen readers, override user settings, and add bloat.

climax.text: Real accessibility requires fixing source code: semantic HTML, ARIA labels, and keyboard support. No shortcut.

punchline.text: No shortcut replaces real accessibility work. Fix the code, not the surface.

quiz.question: Why do accessibility experts oppose overlay tools?
options: Overlays interfere with assistive technology and don't fix the underlying code problems
Overlays are too expensive for small businesses
Overlays only work on mobile browsers
